description: Add light/dark mode toggle to the Claru portal (/portal/* routes) while keeping marketing pages permanently dark. Uses next-themes with CSS variable overrides scoped via .portal-theme-scope class.

userStories.title: Install next-themes and create PortalThemeProvider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the next-themes package installed and a scoped ThemeProvider wrapping the portal layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Run npm install next-themes and verify it appears in package.json dependencies
Create src/app/portal/PortalThemeProvider.tsx as a 'use client' component
PortalThemeProvider wraps children with ThemeProvider from next-themes with: attribute='data-theme', defaultTheme='system', storageKey='claru-portal-theme', enableSystem=true
Update src/app/portal/layout.tsx: import and wrap children with PortalThemeProvider
Update src/app/portal/layout.tsx: add class 'portal-theme-scope' to the wrapper div (the one with min-h-screen)
Marketing pages (/, /case-studies, /privacy, /terms) must NOT have portal-theme-scope class or ThemeProvider
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Portal layout is at src/app/portal/layout.tsx. It wraps all /portal/* routes. The root layout (src/app/layout.tsx) must NOT be modified. next-themes docs: attribute sets the HTML attribute used for theme, storageKey controls localStorage key name.

userStories.title: Define light theme CSS variables in globals.css
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the light theme color palette defined as CSS variable overrides scoped to the portal.
acceptanceCriteria: Add light theme variable overrides in src/app/globals.css using selector: [data-theme='light'] .portal-theme-scope
Override all background variables: --bg-primary: #FAFAF8, --bg-secondary: #F2F1EF, --bg-tertiary: #E8E7E4, --bg-elevated: #FFFFFF, --bg-card: #FFFFFF, --bg-card-hover: #F7F6F4
Override all text variables: --text-primary: #1A1A1A, --text-secondary: rgba(26,26,26,0.8), --text-tertiary: rgba(26,26,26,0.55), --text-muted: rgba(26,26,26,0.4)
Override accent variables: --accent-primary: #5E8A5A, --accent-secondary: #4A7346, --accent-tertiary: #92B090, --accent-glow: rgba(94,138,90,0.15), --accent-glow-strong: rgba(94,138,90,0.3)
Override border variables: --border-subtle: rgba(0,0,0,0.06), --border-medium: rgba(0,0,0,0.12), --border-strong: rgba(0,0,0,0.2), --border-accent: rgba(94,138,90,0.3)
Override semantic colors: --error: #d32f2f, --warning: #f57c00, --success: #5E8A5A
Add smooth transition to .portal-theme-scope: transition: background-color 200ms ease, color 200ms ease
Add scoped ::selection override for light theme: [data-theme='light'] .portal-theme-scope ::selection { background: var(--accent-primary); color: #FFFFFF }
Verify marketing landing page (/) has zero visual changes — inspect in browser
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add the light theme block AFTER the existing :root variables in globals.css. The .portal-theme-scope class was added to portal layout in US-001. CSS specificity: [data-theme='light'] .portal-theme-scope is more specific than :root, so variables inside portal will override correctly.

userStories.title: Add theme toggle button to PortalNav
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want a toggle button in the navigation bar to switch between light and dark modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/portal/PortalNav.tsx to add a theme toggle button
Position the toggle between the nav links (Catalog, Request) and the existing divider/Sign Out section
Add a new divider before the toggle to visually separate it from nav links
Import Sun and Moon icons from lucide-react
Import useTheme from next-themes
Add a mounted state (useState + useEffect) to prevent hydration mismatch — only render the icon after mount
When mounted=false, render a placeholder div with same dimensions (w-8 h-8) to prevent layout shift
Toggle onClick: if theme is 'dark' or 'system' with dark preference, set to 'light'; otherwise set to 'dark'
Show Moon icon when current resolved theme is dark, Sun icon when light
Button styling matches existing nav items: px-2 py-2 rounded-md, text-[var(--text-tertiary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] transition-colors duration-200
Icon size: h-4 w-4 with strokeWidth={1.5} (matching other nav icons)
Add aria-label: 'Switch to light mode' or 'Switch to dark mode' based on current theme
No hydration mismatch warnings in browser console
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: PortalNav.tsx is a 'use client' component at src/app/portal/PortalNav.tsx. useTheme() returns { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }. resolvedTheme resolves 'system' to actual 'light' or 'dark'. The mounted guard pattern: const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false); useEffect(() => setMounted(true), []); — render placeholder until mounted.

userStories.title: Apply theme scope to portal login page
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want the login page to also respect the light/dark theme preference.
acceptanceCriteria: Check if src/app/portal/login/page.tsx is within the portal layout tree (src/app/portal/layout.tsx)
If login page IS within the portal layout tree: it automatically gets portal-theme-scope from layout.tsx — verify this works and no additional changes needed
If login page is NOT within the portal layout tree: wrap its root element with the portal-theme-scope class and import PortalThemeProvider
Login page form elements (email input, submit button, text) all properly styled in both light and dark themes via CSS variables
The login page header text 'claru/portal' with accent slash displays correctly in both themes
The '$ passwordless authentication' footer text is readable in both themes
No hydration issues on login page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The login page is at src/app/portal/login/page.tsx. Since it's under src/app/portal/, it should inherit the portal layout.tsx which now has PortalThemeProvider and portal-theme-scope. Check by navigating to /portal/login and toggling theme. Also check /portal/login path in middleware.ts — it should be accessible without auth.

userStories.title: Light theme scrollbar and form element polish
userStories.description: As a portal user, I want scrollbars and native form elements to look correct in light mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Add scoped scrollbar styles for light theme in globals.css: [data-theme='light'] .portal-theme-scope ::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: var(--bg-secondary) }
[data-theme='light'] .portal-theme-scope ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: var(--border-strong); border-radius: 4px }
[data-theme='light'] .portal-theme-scope ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover { background: var(--text-tertiary) }
Verify select dropdowns (modality dropdown on /portal/request) render with correct background/text colors in light mode
Verify textarea and input focus rings (focus:border-[var(--accent-primary)] focus:ring-[var(--accent-primary)]) work in both themes
Verify error message boxes (bg-[var(--error)]/10 border-[var(--error)]/30) render correctly in light mode
Verify disabled states (opacity-50) look correct in both themes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The request form page is at src/app/portal/request/page.tsx. It has textarea, select, and error message rendering. Navigate to /portal/request and test all form elements in both themes. The /portal/request page may require auth — if so, test by temporarily checking the login page form elements instead.

userStories.title: Visual QA of all portal pages in both themes
userStories.description: As QA, I need to verify all portal pages render correctly in both light and dark modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigate to /portal/login in light mode — verify nav, form, footer all styled correctly
Navigate to /portal/login in dark mode — verify no regressions from current appearance
Navigate to /portal/catalog (if accessible) — verify dataset cards, search bar, filter buttons, tag pills all correct in both themes
Navigate to /portal/request — verify form fields, submit button, 'or' divider, 'Book a call' button, footer text all correct in both themes
Verify the PortalNav theme toggle icon updates correctly when switching themes
Verify theme persists after page navigation between portal pages (e.g. catalog → request → catalog)
Verify navigating to marketing page (/) shows dark theme always, then back to portal shows saved theme
Check browser console for zero hydration mismatch warnings
Check that no portal component has hardcoded color values that break in light mode (search .tsx files under src/app/portal/ for hex color codes like #0a0908, #121110, etc.)
If any hardcoded colors found, fix them to use CSS variables
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the final QA pass. Use Playwright browser automation to navigate and screenshot each page in both themes. Focus on contrast — text should be clearly readable against backgrounds in both themes. Key pages: /portal/login, /portal/catalog, /portal/catalog/[id], /portal/request. Auth may be required for catalog pages.
